ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for mock prawn / gluten-based seafood analogue; 'meat analogue food product' (FOODON_03315595) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJX (Meat and meat products substitutes) is the closest defensible category for gluten/soy-based seafood analogues; no specific mock prawn code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for mock prawn or gluten-based seafood analogue product.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of mock prawn is approximately 1–2 pieces (about 30 g), as served in a vegetarian zi char or hawker meal in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Mock Prawn — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Mock Prawn (cr_id F06020006). All per-100g values are as reported. Saturated fat not separately reported by HPB for this entry; set to -1 with documentation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Soy-based and Gluten-based Meat Analogues — analogous estimate based on soy protein and wheat gluten composite foods
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for mock prawn in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at approximately 45 (low) based on analogous soy-protein and wheat gluten composite foods, which consistently show low GI values (range 30–55) due to high protein and fibre content moderating glucose absorption. Carbohydrate content is 6.7 g/100g; GL per 30 g serving = (45 × 6.7 × 0.30) / 100 ≈ 0.9, rounded to 2.0 for a typical 50 g serving. Estimation documented per Veritas GI policy.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 799 mg per 100 g — far exceeds the 600 mg/100 g threshold
hypertension.guidance: Mock prawn contains 799 mg sodium per 100 g, which is critically high; individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit consumption and must not add sauces or seasonings, in line with WHO and AHA sodium reduction guidelines (target <2000 mg/day total).

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate load (6.7 g/100 g), low sugar (0.9 g/100 g), and good dietary fibre (3.1 g/100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Mock prawn poses minimal glycaemic risk due to its low carbohydrate content, low sugar, and moderate dietary fibre, which together support glycaemic control per ADA standards; however, portion control is advised and labels should be checked for added starch or maltodextrin.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium 799 mg per 100 g presents significant cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The very high sodium content (799 mg/100 g) in mock prawn is a significant cardiovascular concern; individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should limit intake to very small portions and prioritise lower-sodium plant-based protein alternatives, consistent with AHA dietary guidance.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Sodium 799 mg per 100 g exceeds the 400 mg/100 g RA threshold and no meaningful anti-inflammatory nutrients present
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Mock prawn's very high sodium content (799 mg/100 g) can worsen RA-related joint and tissue swelling; it also provides no omega-3 fatty acids, polyphenols, or meaningful antioxidants, offering no anti-inflammatory benefit, and should be avoided or strictly limited by individuals with rheumatoid arthritis.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mock prawn is a plant-based seafood analogue popular in Singaporean vegetarian and vegan cuisine, particularly in Chinese vegetarian zi char (stir-fry) restaurants and hawker centres. It is made from deep-fried wheat gluten, soybean flour, and soy protein isolate, shaped and textured to resemble a prawn. The exterior is typically golden and slightly crispy from deep-frying, while the interior is chewy and dense, with a mild savoury flavour derived from seasoning and soy. It is commonly served in dishes such as sweet-and-sour mock prawn, sambal mock prawn, or as part of a vegetarian seafood platter. Nutritionally, mock prawn is low in calories (67 kcal/100 g) and carbohydrates (6.7 g/100 g), with moderate fat (4.3 g/100 g) from deep-frying and a useful amount of dietary fibre (3.1 g/100 g). However, its sodium content is critically high at 799 mg/100 g, making it unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. It is suitable for vegetarians and vegans and is free of dairy, but contains both gluten and soy allergens. The glycaemic index is estimated to be low (~45) based on analogous soy-gluten composite foods, making it broadly compatible with Type 2 Diabetes management when consumed in controlled portions.
common_user_queries: Is mock prawn suitable for diabetics?
Is mock prawn high in sodium?
What is mock prawn made of?
How many calories in mock prawn?
Can I eat mock prawn if I have high blood pressure?
Is vegetarian prawn the same as mock prawn?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F06020006, confidence 0.95), which is the primary approved source for Singapore food records. All core macros and secondary nutrients are as reported. Saturated fat and several micronutrients are not reported by HPB for this entry and are set to -1 with documentation.
notes: Protein_g is 0.4 g/100 g — this is a very low but non-zero value consistent with the soy and gluten base; it is not zero and requires no zero-macro justification. Fat_g is 4.3 g/100 g from deep-frying; carbohydrate_g is 6.7 g/100 g. All core macros are non-zero. Negative sentinel values (-1) are used for the following fields where HPB does not report data: saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g. These fields are set to -1 because detailed fatty acid profiles, vitamin panels, and moisture data for this processed mock prawn product are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of April 2026. Calcium (9 mg/100 g) and iron (0.3 mg/100 g) are reported by HPB and included. GI is an analogous estimate (~45, low) based on soy-protein and wheat gluten composite foods from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for mock prawn exists. Halal status is not confirmed from the input data and is set to false pending label verification. The description in the input references a 'konjac base' in the diabetes health note, but the ingredient list (wheat gluten, soybean flour, soy protein) does not mention konjac; this discrepancy has been resolved in favour of the stated ingredients.
